artJewelleryItems.11: Encolpion - Medallion with circle or cross motif

artGemstoneTypes.11: A mix of emeralds and sapphires
artGemstoneTypes.17: A blend of rubies, emeralds, and sapphires
artGemstoneTypes.44: Alternating coral beads and jade stones
artGemstoneTypes.47: A mix of lapis lazuli and malachite stones

hooks1: Prevent someone else from finding lost lore
On the lam - running from the law
Help a new ruler come to power
hooks2: News spreads that a dragon was slain while away from his cave, a search for its unguarded hoard is underway
A thief has stolen a powerful item from a lich and then joins the party for protection
A wizard/collector wants a live troll to study
Rat catchers are going missing under the city and a plague is spreading in their absence
A local caster has summoned a creature that they cannot contain and it is destroying the area
A charismatic charlatan claims (and has proof) to be the cousin/brother/son of one of the PCs
A wandering merchant trades a PC for their magic item for a fake he claims is more powerful
Shipments from a nearby mine have stopped when the PCs investigate they find the miners crazed and covered with red welts apparently from exposure to a new element they uncovered
A white dragon is driving monsters from the north into the southern lands
A planar rift has formed and outsiders are seeping through
A powerful noble/wizard is hosting a masquerade ball where the guests are polymorphed into monsters as their costume, but an actual monster attends to kill the noble/wizard
A sorcerer has died of old age, strange things are creeping out of his tower as his spells and dweomers break down
A map has been found that leads to parts of an artifact that once reassembled, will summon a fiendish kraken
The dead are rising as zombies one hour after their death
Slavers are capturing peasants and merchants on the highway and selling them to mindflayers
A shop/traveling merchant sells pets/familiars that are actually polymorphed people
The PCs are sent to find a hermit that lives on the 'moving island', a zircon/dragon turtle
A traveling circus/faire comes to town and completely vanishes in the morning with several children
An Ur priest cult is killing all the divine casters in the area
Water drawn from a certain well is animating into water elementals/mephits
An evil druid has taken up residence in the sewers and is waging a guerilla war on civilization
Anyone who reads a cursed book, brought into town by an adventuring party, dies after reading it
A killer is leaving rare flowers in the mouths of his victims
A member of a planar cartographical society offers the PCs membership into the elite group if they can complete a scavenger hunt that leads them across several planes in one day

A mysterious helmed/hooded/masked figure has forged an impressive army by bringing tribes of kobolds, goblins, orcs, and hobgoblins under one banner. The leader is actually a cleric disheartened by the lack of faith and respect of his flock and means to increase belief and prayer for his deity by leading an army of wolves against his flock
The owner of a failing inn claims to have the entrance to a mysterious dungeon in his cellar hoping that the ruse will draw business from adventurers
A pack of displacer beasts/displacer beast lord is preying on farm animals and farmers alike
Two rival gangs are actually devils and demons fighting a Blood War battle on the city streets

The daughter/apprentice of a caster that polymorphed himself into a golden cup and placed himself in the hoard of a green dragon in an attempt to learn more about dragons, but it has been weeks and he has not come home contacts the PCs
Reports that a gold dragon is ravaging the countryside turn out to be true. The dragon, sick with a rare disease, has gone mad and must be stopped
Grave robbers working for a necromancer are running out of graves and start looking for easy prey
A 'red' dragon demanding tributes from a village is actually an especially greedy copper dragon
A killer is released from prison and the father of one of his victims stages a similar murder in the hopes of framing him
A traveling 'holy man' is selling relics that disappear in the morning
The new judge is in fact a devil hoping to harvest souls for not guilty verdicts
A logging camp is being haunted by the ghost of a treant/forest haunt and his dryad followers
A band of fey has been stealing wine from a rural tavern
A good and helpful aranea has been captured and tried for murder while the true culprit is a drider that resides close by
A college that teaches science over magic opens and arcane casters start going missing
Drug-related deaths lead the PCs to an evil alchemist
The PCs are hired to retrieve a meteor, but find it is being worshipped by a tribe of goblins/orcs/lizardmen etc.
Several women in the area are pregnant under strange circumstances, an incubus is to blame
An artifact is needed to avert a major catastrophe; its last known owner was Levistus the arch devil trapped within a glacier
The PCs must find a rare herb only known to grow within Gith monasteries
The PCs visit a strange village where all the people are simulacrums, an ancient wizard's mark is seen everywhere
The PCs are sent to a distant land to find an NPC that it turns out has been dead for 20 years
The PCs must help a conflicted Erinyes to the Cradle of Creation to be reborn into a non-evil body

A map leading to the legendary Shield of Praetor has been found, it states that the shield is in the cave of a dracolich. The map was sent by the dracolich’s minions in the hopes of freeing their master who is sealed magically into his cave
A local orphanage is actually run by a demonic cultist that sacrifices the weak children and raises the strong ones to be followers
A powerful artifact that will allow teleportation through the layers of the abyss/hells has been uncovered and a race to claim it has begun
An overmatched Marut seeks assistance with a powerful lich/vampire/mummy
Rumors of 'The most powerful sword' lead the PCs to an evil, intelligent, dancing sword that can animate other weapons to fight for it
The PCs are sent to a battlefield to retrieve a family heirloom from a missing soldier where a necromancer and his corpse collector golem are raising the fallen as undead
A cult of Tiamat have discovered a spell that ages living creatures and they are trying to use it on dragon eggs to raise powerful allies
The PCs are sent to deal with a raiding ogre that turns out to be a gnome illusionist
The PCs are looking for an arrow used to slay a dragon a hundred years earlier, but when they pull the arrow from the dragon’s skeleton, it animates and attacks
The PCs need the help/information of a treant that will only aid them if they agree to be shrunk down to clear out an infestation of insects that invaded his roots
The PCs are invited to a Three Dragon Ante tourney, either as guards, VIPs, or to play
A lawful good lich seeks the PCs to protect him from a zealous paladin on a quest to rid the world of undead
Thieves have plundered a tomb and until his golden burial mask is returned, a ghost/ghast/specter will continue to kill innocent people
A group of fire giants has taken up residence in an inactive volcano, their activity threatens to awaken the volcano and cause widespread devastation

A grandmotherly, if slightly senile, NPC asks the players to rid her attic of rats. The rats are in fact a group of thieves trying to open a magical doorway left by the wizard that previously owned the home
A monster seen roaming close to town is actually a cursed person and not evil
The PCs find a genie in a bottle, but the genie agrees to help/grant wishes/serve only after the players travel to the City of Brass and save someone the genie cares about
The PCs find a wounded angel that is being hunted by powerful outsiders
A newly discovered dungeon is actually a complex trap to harvest souls/magic/life energy
The PCs must break an innocent man from a complex magical prison
Murders attributed to a small girl are being done by her doll, a slaymate
An ancient beholder has gone mad and is destroying the Underdark, driving monsters to the surface
A gnome settlement has been overrun by Drow displacing hundreds of citizens
A mad wizard has been selling potions that have poisonous/odd effects
Mind flayers are draining people of their quintessence in hopes of using the substance to return to the far realm from which aberrations came
A spelljammer has crashed in a remote forest/jungle and the inhabitants seek materials to repair their helm
A foreign diplomat seeks the party’s monk to protect him on a mission to a country/city where magic and weapons are not allowed

The tarrasque is wreaking havoc on the countryside and the party (level 10ish) must slow it down until the champions (20th level) can arrive, but the tarrasque is actually a simulacrum (CR 10) sent by an outsider/caster/etc. and not the real thing
Cultists seek a tablet that depicts a ritual that will summon a Fist of Spite
The party must save an NPC from the stomach demiplane of Dalmosh
The guild master of the cooper’s guild wants to discredit the owner of a local winery with whom he has had an argument by poisoning his barrels
A local sage/astronomer is convinced that a massive meteor is going to strike the kingdom/city/town
The normally-inert gargoyles atop the temple/castle/mansion have animated and started attacking people who approach the building
A group of bulettes is keeping anyone from entering or leaving the city/town/inn
A doppelganger/changeling serial killer claims the identity of their most recent victim for one week before killing again
A gnome settlement has been overrun by fiendish duergar led by a demon
PCs seek out a powerful dwarven smith who traded his soul to Asmodeus for unearthly crafting abilities. Before the smith will help the PCs, they must reclaim his soul from the arch devil
The players find/are sent to a city that reflects the entire multiverse scaled down with a neutral inn in the center run by a powerful caster
The PCs search for a legendary library that, when found, has no books — only the corpses of long-dead sages and librarian clerics who use speak with dead to provide knowledge
The PCs need a party member/NPC raised from the dead but the only cleric powerful enough to do so has recently been turned by a vampire he was hunting

The PCs find/buy/are given a strange bag of holding that has a small pocket dimension inside it where a frightened caster hides. He/she created the bag to hide in and saw that it ended up in the PCs' hands to keep it safe
The PCs seek an answer/information from a forgotten bard. When they find him, he is a ghost and he will only help them if they give him peace by finishing his final poem/song/movement
A chaotic good horselord has led all the horses in the region/city/town away into the hills to freedom
The huntsman of a local lord/mayor has kidnapped the NPC’s daughter, and only a highly trained tracker can follow the trail and find the girl
An aged and grizzled warrior is going town to town offering his magic sword/shield/armor to any fighter that can best him in honorable combat

After returning from a diplomatic journey, the noble/diplomat/prince/queen is acting strangely. The PCs are asked to look into it only to find that the NPC is a doppelganger/changeling/simulacrum/charmed/possessed
A pair of ethereal filchers are stealing all the curative magic in the area/city/town
The answer/riddle/name/code that the PCs require is etched onto the helm of a massive golem that paces a deadly dungeon
Monthly full moon attacks are blamed on a good lycanthrope, and are actually being carried out by a pack of Moon Rats
Summoned Thoqqua threaten to compromise the structural integrity of the city/town/inn/dungeon as they melt tunnels through the ground below
Centaur knights are running any humanoid from their forest
The PCs are sought by the patrons of a desert land where a despotic temple of clerics charges impossibly high rates for fresh water to people not of their faith

A temple has hired a large number of bards for a festival where music is to be played from sun up until sundown — the only problem is that no one recalls the obscure holiday because it is a ruse to mask the sound of tomb robbers breaking into sealed vaults below the church
A xenophobic elvish lord has begun to arrest non-elves after his daughter eloped with a human
goals: To reunite with a loved one
To survive just one more day
To be smarter, faster and stronger
To hide from a shameful fact
To make good on a bet
To get out of an obligation
To convince someone to do their dirty work
To save someone from a monstrosity
To get just one more haul
To do what must be done
To become a terror to criminals
To do what can’t be done
To make the best of it
To not screw it all up
To go where no man has gone before
To hunt the most dangerous game
villainGoals: Acquire a legendary item to prolong life
Ascend to godhood
Become undead or obtain a younger body
Steal a planar creature's essence
Seize a position of power or title
Win a contest or tournament
Win favor with a powerful individual
Place a pawn in a position of power
Obtain an ancient artifact
Build a construct or magical device
Carry out a deity's wishes
Offer sacrifices to a deity
Contact a lost deity or power
Open a gate to another world
Fulfill an apocalyptic prophecy
Enact the vengeful will of a god or patron
Spread a vile contagion
Overthrow a government
Trigger a natural disaster
Utterly destroy a bloodline or clan
Prolong the life of a loved one
Prove worthy of another person's love
Raise or restore a dead loved one
Destroy rivals for another person's affection
Conquer a region or incite a rebellion
Seize control of an army
Become the power behind the throne
Gain the favor of a ruler
Avenge a past humiliation or insult
Avenge a past imprisonment or injury
Avenge the death of a loved one
Retrieve stolen property and punish the thief
Control natural resources or trade
Marry into wealth
Plunder ancient ruins
Steal land, goods, or money
specialAdvantage: A plan for the perfect crime
A sense of right and wrong
A hidden entrance to the city
A key to an important door
An arrow with your name on it
A book in a strange language
The wind at his back and a spring in his step
generalActivityTable: Doing everyday task: laundry, prepping food, melting coins, licking wounds

01-18.question: Who lives, visits, or has ties here?

19-29.question: What does this place produce or consume?

30-38.question: Who knows something about this place?

39-45.question: Who controls or influences this place?

46-52.question: Who is competing with or fighting over it?

61-64.question: What work is done for someone else here?

65-71.question: What does this place depend upon? Who relies on it?

72-78.question: What past event links this place to others? What has been left behind?

85-87.question: What religious or cultural ties exist?

88-91.question: How does the environment connect places?

99.question: Who or what regularly travels here?

retainerReason: Addicted to an Expensive/Illicit Substance and needs to support their Habit
Barfight got out of hand, and now Wergild is due
Bear-Baiting has grown a tad Dangerous for their liking
Being Blackmailed to Participate by Villainous Local Forces
Better to Skip Town before a recent Crime they committed comes to light
Bilked out of their Mortgage by a Snake Oil Salesman
Blacklisted by local Scandal: No one else is Buying what they're Selling
Blamed for recent Crop Failure, wants to start anew in another Town
Business closed due to stiff Competition: Time for a new Line of Work
Buying back Ancestral Lands from Greedy Lords takes considerable Coin
Can't even afford the Flophouse for a Night, desperate for a place to Sleep
Caught Poaching Rabbit to feed their Family: Exorbitant Fines
Close Friend fell into the River and Funerals are expensive
Commitment made while they were quite Intoxicated, but their Word is bond
Convinced by the Fae that they are Destined for Heroic Greatness
Cousin struck it Rich in this way, they're hoping for a similar Windfall
Craves the Finer Things in Life: Fancy Fabrics and Foods
Crop failed and Coffers are low: Maybe earn enough to make it through Winter
Day In, Day Out: Always the same. Desperately in need of Novelty
Deemed far too Clumsy or Talentless for their Family's Trade
Deeply Religious, wants to help fund the new Shrine going up
Desires Vengeance on a type of Monster for slaying their Kin
Desperate and Destitute after their Spouse ran away with their Savings
Disowned and Disinherited from their Family, trying to make ends meet
Drank away their Life's Savings to drown out a Broken Heart
Dying Wish of Family Member was to have their Ashes scattered There
Encounter with a Wight has left them Drained but seeking Revenge
Escaping the Back-Breaking, uncompensated Labor of Apprenticeship
Evicted from their Rooms in the Inn for non-payment
Expecting a Child, and needs a Nest Egg
Faithful, Three-Legged Hound went Missing in the area a week back
Family Member has an Illness/Medical Condition: Treatment expensive
Fell Head-Over-Heels for Someone, wants to shower them with Gifts/Riches
Fiddle is in Hock, hoping to earn enough to buy it back and return to Music
Fond of making wagers they're wont to welch on, piper paying time arrived
Fortune Teller promised there'd Silver galore from this endeavor
Found an Expensive Looking Gem, but needs money for Appraisal
Fresh out of the Orphanage with vanishingly few Prospects
Fugitive from Justice, adopting an Assumed Name/Disguise
Funds required to pay for a Mentor for Schooling in the ways of Magic
Futility of that Daily Grind finally got to them: Must be a Better Way
Got Mixed Up with Organized Crime, Leg Breakers don't take IOUs
Has seen the way Townsfolk treat "Heroes" and wants in on that Action
Haunted by a Ghost that they hope to lay to Rest during this Jaunt
Hired to Spy on the Party by Rival Adventurers
Home burned down in a recent Fire, needs funds to Rebuild
Hoping to earn enough to buy that Perfect Present for their Grandmother
Horrible Flood/Weather left them bereft of Possessions or place to live
Injury has rendered them unsuitable for their Usual Line of Work
Insatiable Appetite risks putting them in the Poorhouse
It was either this or Take the Cloth, and they’re not particularly Pious
Just-Minted, Probationary Member of the Torchbearer's Guild
Kicked out of a Local Guild/Social Club for not paying their Dues
Known to other Townsfolk as a bit of a Daredevil/Attention-Seeker
Letter from Relative urgently requesting aid: Needs money for Travel
Lost a Loved One, hoping to pay the Steep Price to have them Brought Back
Made a Bet they would, and Lost but trying to stay true to their Word
Mistreated Horse is for Sale, and they desperately want to Buy/Care for her
Needs to gather Funds to Remove an Inconvenient Hex or Curse
Never Quite "Fit In" with the Locals, hailing from far away Lands
New Mill was opened, and their water Diverted
Old Ox keeled over and died during Harvest Season, got to buy a new one
Only around a dozen Gold Pieces shy of getting that Elaborate New Tattoo
Parceled out Land a few Days Ride away is going for Cheap
Parents were Adventurers, seeks to follow in their Footsteps
Perhaps storing their Savings in their Mattress wasn't the Wisest idea
Personal Tragedy has led them to engage in Risk-taking Behaviors
Pestilence wiped out their Livestock, got to get a new Breeding Pair
Pining for the Excitement of the Big City, but needs to get there first
Political Ambitions and wishes to ameliorate Reputation
Read or Listened to far too many Exciting Tales of Daring-Do
Recently dismissed from Servitude/freed from Jail, making up for lost time
Recurring Dreams of glittering Hoards urge them to assume Risks
Rite of Passage to join the Town Guard, prove your Mettle
Saving up for a Dowry to have a chance at Marriage
Saw some Adventurers splash their Coin about, wants in on the action
Seeking to disprove swirling Rumors of Cowardice in an extreme way
Series of bad Financial Choices have put them in Arrears to Local Lord
Sheltered up-bringing and desperate to shed those Shackles
Since they were Young, was always fascinated with tales of Magical Beasts
Spouse/Parent/Dependent ran up a huge Tab at the Tavern that's come Due
Swore up and down, Never Again. But here they are
Tales from the Troubadours make it seem so Easy: Get Rich Quick!
Teased by a Local Bully, and seeks to prove themselves
Their Pet is a remarkably Picky Eater
Thrill-seeking is apparently a bit of a Family Trait for them
Too Proud for Handouts, and Odd Jobs are growing Scarce
Tradition dictates that they participate in this before Settling Down
Travelling Circus skipped Town without 'em
Two things Certain: Death and Taxes, they'll find out which soon enough
Ulterior Motive: Family Member went Missing recently: Hopes to find them
Upcoming Duel of Honor has them itching to get some Combat Practice in
Vowed to care for their Siblings after losing their Parents to Plague
Wanderlust: There must be more than this boring, One Warhorse Town
Wanted for stealing Chickens, this might be a way to Lay Low
Weddings aren't Cheap in this neck of the Woods
Wishes to partake in a Pilgrimage, but knows there's safety in Numbers
Wolves took their Flock, one-by-one, hateful things
Yearns for nothing less than showing up a Childhood Rival
Youngest from a very large Family with vanishingly few Prospects
retainerQuirks: Absent-mindedly fddles with the Copper Locket that graces their Neck
Adamant about getting Plenty of Sleep, dibs on First or Last Watch
Always a bit to eager to Draw Lots/Randomly Determine Volunteers
Avoids getting Wet, going to comical lengths sometimes
Bad with Distances/Volumes/Weights, always under-estimates
Believes that one day, they're destined to be slain by a Skeleton
Breath that would make a Troglodyte Flinch
Carries a rather foul smelling, but delicious Condiment: Puts it on everything
Chewing on something, constantly, like a Cow
Childhood Friend Drowned, especially wary around Water
Claims to be descended from a Powerful Druidess
Come to think of it, you've never seen them Eat
Complains Food is Too Salty or Not Salty enough, sometimes same dish
Considers the color Green to be "Lucky," goes out of their way to prove this
Constantly picking up Smooth Stones for Skipping, their record is 38
Convinced that their Beltpouch is Haunted after a Vivid Dream
Critical of Underhanded or Sneaky Tactics, sees them as Cowardly
Cultivates a Mysterious Aura, badly: Smiles and Giggles too much
Despises Waste, doesn't like to discard or throw things away
Doesn't much care for Animals, not callous or cruel, just disdainful
Doodles on Walls with a Charcoal Nib wherever they go
Eager to show you a rather Gruesome Scar, how they got it tends to change
Eats very decorously and elegantly, regardless of conditions
Especially Jumpy around Giant Animals/Insects
Excessively dignified and old-fashioned around Elfs and Dwarfs
Falls in and out of Love at the drop of a Helm
Fascinated by Owls: Sees them as a Portentous Beast
Fasts on specific Holy Days, but doesn't make a big production out of it
Feeding their pet Raven is always very Loud and Messy
Fleet of Foot, provided they're not overladen, so very prone to dropping
Fond of repeating the Expression: "Good enough for Goblins"
Formerly a Sailor and critical of Rope Use and Knots
Four changes of Clothes are Carried at all times, this is non-negotiable
Gossips a lot about People you're not likely to ever meet
Grumbles at the slightest sign of Inclement Weather, feels it in bones
Horses/Mules just hate them, could be that Hippogriff Smell
Insists on checking behind every Curtain, overturning every Rug
Invariably Itchy: Scratches themselves a lot, often making moaning sounds
Is never without a String of Dried Mushrooms of various species
Keeps, at last count, over a dozen Cats, often rattles off their Names
Knack for inadvertently stumbling into the Mechanisms for Secret Doors
Knows the Recipe for a Healing Tea (disgusting) of dubious efficacy
Knuckles crack like Popcorn: Often and Loudly
Laments the lack of a Comfortable Place to Sleep at every opportunity
Likes to have everything formalized in writing, even though they're illiterate
Lost Several Years to a Dryad's Charms, nervous in the Woods
Maintains that they are completely Immune to Magic, at all times
Makes a Superstitious Gesture any time anyone utters a Monster Name
Mends things, always seems to be fixing this that or the other
Missing an Eye from a Childhood Run-in with Malevolent Pixies
Much ado about Birthdays, Namedays, and other assorted Holidays
Nearly always Gives Alms to Beggars or those Down on their Luck
Never met a Fire they couldn't Light on the First Try, so they say
Often mentions how a prior group of Companions met their Grisly End
One arm covered in Brand Marks that they refuse to discuss
Opposite of a Picky Eater, constantly scrounging: "You gonna eat that?"
Overly Chummy, greets nearly anyone with "Howdy Friend!"
Pacer, has a difficult time standing still for too long
Prays before every Meal and glares at those who don't while doing so
Preens whenever they see a Mirror or Reflective Surface
Prefers to be paid in Electrum for Spiritual Reasons
Protests if asked to be the first to enter an Unexplored Area
Punctuates nearly every utterance with "y'know?"
Quick to Swear Oaths over sometimes very inconsequential things
Quotes Holy Verses that are seldom applicable to the Situation at hand
Rarely Complains, unless it's Cold...then it's non-stop
Refuses to take Direct Orders from anyone with Brown Eyes
Rolls their Eyes when Plans take too long to discuss: Craves Action
Says Second Sight runs in the Family: Insists they see Ghosts
Seeks Trophies and Mementos from every Expedition
Seems to Lose their Knife/Dagger almost constantly
Sheepishly averts their eyes whenever a Grave or Dead Body is present
Sniffs and Sneezes quite often, must be the Dust/Mold
Somehow Learned to Read about 17 languages, badly
Spits on Shields before a Battle, unsure where they picked that up
Strokes their Chin when asked a Question, as if pondering a response
Swears up and down that they can Witch for Water if given a Twig
Talented Camp Cook, food is always delicious somehow
Talks to their Gear/Equipment when they suspect no one is listening
Tallies Monsters Slain via notches, makes Offerings for each in secret
Tends to whistle (very off-key) when nervous or trying to be quiet
Thinks it's Bad Luck to not drain a Tankard/Skin on the First Draught
Thoughtfully lets others Sleep in a little on their Watches
Total Teetotaler, until someone turns their back
Treats minor injuries by whacking them with the sole of a Boot
Tries to work in the story of how they saw a Dragon once, constantly
Triple Checks Buttons, Clasps, and Straps
Urges everyone to dry Feet each Night by a Fire, theirs are unpleasant
Uses a Sharp Knife to uncouthly pick their Nails or Teeth
Utterly Refuses to Climb anything higher than their Height
Vehemently Asserts that Torches be carried "on the Right"
Very bad with Names, even mixing up Party Members
Vociferously Vocal about Unusual Dietary Restriction/Taboo
Was swindled by a Gnome, has been distrustful of them ever since
Wears Boots that are far too big and tend to squeak when they walk
When asked to lift anything Heavy, complains about their Bad Back
Whittles Wooden Game Pieces in spare time, some are quite good
Won't take off that Dented Helm, mutters platitudes when asked
Woolgathers aloud about what to spend their inevitable Fortune on
Wrinkles their Nose when offered a Compliment
